Crystasia's Crystal Ring  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FINGER
AC: 30
HP: +100 MANA: +100 ENDUR: +100
SV FIRE: +10 SV DISEASE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10 SV POISON: +10
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Crystasia's Crystal Ring
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FINGER
AC: 30
HP: +100 MANA: +100 ENDUR: +100
SV FIRE: +10 SV DISEASE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10 SV POISON: +10
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Lore:Ring of the Chosen
Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:lowkey
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-11 08:55:54
Page Updated:Sun Oct 3rd, 2021

*This item is the result of a raid event.
Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 65
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Temple of Veeshan
NPC Name
The Fabled Vulak`Aerr
Vulak`Aerr


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Temple of Veeshan

Great ring for every class
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2002 at 1:56 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591781544113
2 posts
this is one of the many new drops that is amazing for any class, which makes it very difficult for raid leaders to distribute accordingly. I believe considering the mana it should not goto a warrior.
▲ Page top
A little possible help
QuoteReply
# Mar 13 2002 at 9:35 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591774933852
13 posts
I would say this would go to Warrior, Shaman, or Necro first, possibly a monk, the 2 guilds on my server able to take out Vulak have givin this to thier necromancers first. I really dont believe anything all/all should be dedicated to a certain class but it is :( sorry.
▲ Page top
RE: A little possible help
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2002 at 12:39 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966096116
3 posts
Why you would give this to a War or a Monk is beyond me. You'd be wasting that 100mana. This is definetly a SK, PAL, or priest/caster class.
▲ Page top
RE: A little possible help
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2003 at 6:51 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763068787
44 posts
Because the +mana is only a single aspect of the item. It is also 30 ac in the RING slot, +15 to ALL resists and an additional 100hps, again in a RING slot. To try to exclude straight warriors in order for yer toon to get the item seems a wee bit.. shall we say.. greedy?

It's about like saying INT should not use it just becuase it has +30 ac on it.

It is all classes, has stats that all classes can use, therefor any and all classes can use it and should be given a shot at it.

-me
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Feb 09 2002 at 10:41 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592791901862
19 posts
this is to all the people saying main tank or raid leader will loot to assign ownership well if they loot it is theirs END OF STORY NO DROP unless you found some way to give NO DROP items away then STFU :) have a nice day.......

53rd Heretic
Torvonnilous
▲ Page top
RE: OMG NO DROP
QuoteReply
# May 12 2002 at 5:16 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595780146000
99 posts
Yeah, that's called ninja looting. Nobody with half a brain would do that because they would never get into a high level raid ever again in the life of that character. Everytime they shouted "Soandso LFG" there'd be 87 more that said "Don't group with Soandso! He's a ninja looter! He'll ***** over your group!"

Simple logic prevails, yet again.
▲ Page top
RE: OMG NO DROP
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2002 at 4:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779140129
View User Forum
206 posts
I always thought ninja looting was the art of lotting a mob before the carcass hits the ground.
▲ Page top
RE: OMG NO DROP
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2003 at 6:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763068787
44 posts
Yes and no, Ninja looting is basically looting something off of a mob that is not yours to loot, whether the body ever hit the floor or not. It can occur even after the mob has been sitting there for a moment while rolls/DKP bids are being made.

The "art" of looting a corpse before it hits the ground is called being a greedy, platinum-sucking loot *****. =)

-me
▲ Page top
RE: OMG NO DROP
QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2003 at 11:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257949517
35 posts
now i wouldnt go that far, as a druid(or any class that solos for a good portion of their characters life) sitting around killing single or quadding mobs gets a little boring, and one way to make the soloing interesting is playing stupid little games like seeing if you CAN loot a corpse before it hits the ground, then once youve mastered looting before the corpse hits the ground, see how many times you can do it in a row =)
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 15 2001 at 9:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ~~~~~MONK LEWT~~~~~
best class
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2001 at 1:42 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248793200
54 posts
cleric ring.
▲ Page top
RE: best class
QuoteReply
# Dec 02 2001 at 11:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249716241
Sage
207 posts
Umm, begyerpardon?

This is the second highest resist ring in the game and the other one's lore.

It's also All/All

rather than play "what class deserves this item," a game I for one am sick to death of, why not just roll on the damn thing?

Casters over the int cap need resists and mana, this has both. But wait! with 30 ac the reists and the hp, tanks could use it! but WAIT WITH THE LOW WEIGHT IT MUST BE A MONK ITEM!

Just roll on it like rational human beings. That's what /random is in the game for. To keep us from eachother's throats.

Regardless of your class, this is one of the best rings in the game. For resists there is only one better finger item (currently), and it's lore.

Rather than speculate on what class deserves the item, just roll on it. Or decide who gets it beforehand. Just skip the school-yard bickering.


Faelawen Starfyre, The Mad Mage of Povar
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 26 2001 at 7:58 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) tank ring
Vulak'Aerr Drops
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2001 at 7:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249352913
60 posts
Hail, Friends!

It may seem odd that I post it here where the ring is stated, but I want to tell you my thoughts about all the armor and jewelery Vulak'Aerr drops.

I am a Paladin (and a very young one) but I love to look at all the available items for my class. So I scrolled down to see the best items. And I only saw once an item with a better AC than the one Vulak'Aerr drops (At least for a Paladin). That is a Silver Bracelet of Speed (Wrist Slot Item).

Every item he drops has high resists, the highest AC for that equipment slot (excluding the silver bracelet), most of the times HP's, Mana, Wisdom, Intelligence and Strength. Often enough the items have all there is.

I don't dare to try to tell you who should loot this item or which class should roll for it. I think every class out there would love to have a piece of Vulak'Aerr's belongings and it would only be fair that one can not have two if not all others on the Raid/in the Guild have one. And it is great for every class, I do not think these are melee only or hybrid only or caster only items. Pray to your god and roll your dices.

This post had no useful information in it, I know :)

Sorry for boring you and good luck for getting a piece of Vulak'Aerr.
▲ Page top
l
QuoteReply
# Oct 06 2001 at 1:40 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591769558193
61 posts
this by far a hybrid ring look all those resist whogets nuked morethe caster or the tanks/hybrid? also the hp great for a rnager who vernat made under powered the ac grwat anyone the mana that isthe onlythingthat points this the caster direction but no it should go to someoen who can use every one of those stats to the best a hybrid
▲ Page top
ALL/ALL
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2001 at 9:39 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767065550
12 posts
Hey look! See that thing that says ALL/ALL? That means Verant designed it with every class in mind! See, if it were for just one class, it would have specifications as to which class that would be.

And, to get right down to it, when you're level 60 and part of a huge raiding party, one tiny item like this won't mean jack. It's just a status symbol that raises some resists and knocks your AC up.. You're still gonna get quad sliced for over 900 :P
▲ Page top
loot
QuoteReply
# Aug 19 2001 at 6:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1652055378273
24 posts
This would be looted by the guilds "main tank". This is an increase of 20-24 item ac over what they would have before, which would be a stat increase of 30-36ac. Any guild would give this to nobody but their main tank.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 05 2001 at 10:36 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Thank you for your input, but 100 mana does a wizard good. You schmuk. And the resists are pretty good as well.

RE: loot
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2001 at 12:48 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750752940
55 posts
This would be looted by whomever the raid leader decided would loot it. If he/she felt like giving to a druid/bard/SK, etc. they could. Please, items like this will be awarded by the raid leader, no matter what the people on Allakhazam message board say about who should or shouldn't get it. If you have something important to say about the item post it here. If you are just going to start another one of those extremely tired and boring conversation about who is always going to get what uber item, please go somewhere else. Its a waste of time, and i doubt very much that Vulak'Aerr raid leaders come to this site to decide who they are goign to give the drops too. taking that into consideration; posts saying what classes should get EXTREMELY high end items like this are a waste of space, because frankly, your opinion doesn't matter.

dragonbane
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2001 at 4:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766177573
2 posts
Only problem is, he's not db'able
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Aug 28 2001 at 12:34 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Dude, yes he is...shut up, you clueless gimp
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 16 2001 at 12:57 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is truly a great item. Definitely not for monks. Monks can go suck a big one for this thing. Dont forget who is doing all the dmg here, rogue/wizard. Rogues do a hella alot more dmg than monks and wizards own all with Dragonbanes. Noone in their right minds would try to do this dragon, who you have to clear the entire north wing to pull without having at least 5 Dragonbaners (wizards). AT LEAST 5. We do a crapload of dmg, more than any other other class, we get put with a bard, have PoG, GoB, C2 and mana song and we outdo any of you melee class and you all know it. who is going to be protecting us while we do our heavy dmg? it wont be the monks, believe me. it will be the heavily pimped out warriors. monks have other nice items that they can wear, no not the 6ac, 65hp rings but how about ring of the sky or something? thats a really nice monk item.
#Anonymous, Posted: Sep 09 2001 at 3:45 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) hmm.. so a wizard with, about 3000 mana, can cast this spell 6 or 7 times before going oom
RE: NOT FOR MONK
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2001 at 4:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245865037
7 posts
I did 108000dmg to Vulak Aerr.

Im a wizard 60, I have about 3500mana and you REALLY underestimate the power of:

Protection of the Glade
Manasong 32
Cantana of Replenishment
Clarity 2
Barrier of Force
Gift of Brillance
and 2 necromancers feeding you as well as a few modulation rods.

Find me any melee that can do the dmg I do on vulak, specially once theyre slowed to 60% of their normal speed, yes it cancels haste, and Ill call that melee my God.
▲ Page top
RE: NOT FOR MONK
QuoteReply
# Dec 28 2001 at 9:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570536619
1 post
And how many times did you get hit? Mmmm hmm, dealing damage is one thing. Tanking is another. You deal the damage while the tank does his job. Pretty simple if you ask me. It takes all kinds to take on a large mob.

Btw, It is YOUR job to deal damage, it is the tanks job to take the damage. Don't flip out cause you finally figured out what your role is. The tanks knew all along.
▲ Page top
RE: NOT FOR MONK
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2003 at 8:30 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1618186616419
146 posts
Very true.
Wizards deal spell dmg. and alot of it.
Monk deal melee dmg. Ok, wizards to more dmg than monks, but then again, I don't see too many wizards being used for scouting mobs either. Sure, monks aren;'t the only class that can FD pull, but I sure love playing a monk.

Its only a game, and stop putting down other classes. Hmm......I don't see too many wizards giving the main tank a battle rez, cause the secondary tank is 3 levels lower and taking up too much mana from the clerics...
▲ Page top
RE: NOT FOR MONK
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2001 at 6:48 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249435638
10 posts
Aargh, what the hell man? I hate people like you that say "this is definitely not for <insertclasshere>" but I also hate people to say "this is a <insertclasshere> item it should be <insertclasshere> only!"

Okay, the other things I would like to say are:
#1. Monks are no skimpy damage dealers!
#2. Just because you say so doesn't mean it's true!
and above all...
#3. What are you fighting for? What IS THE POINT of saying how much Wizards kick *** and how much rogues out damage monks? What I'm trying to say is...YOU OPINION MEANS NOTHING AND YOUR POST WAS POINTLESS! Aarrgh.

This item is good for ANY CLASS, okay?

Just my 2 cents. I'm getting weary of people that 'decide' what people should get what items.
▲ Page top
Wow
QuoteReply
# Aug 16 2001 at 11:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764386908
6 posts
Wow, I do believe I just found the future upgrade to my Velium Fire Wedding Ring. As a Monk, I wear HP rings on each finger, a Djarn's Amethyst Ring on one, the VFWR on the other and this ring blows away the 6 AC +65 HP's and moreover, resists! Ya gotta love those... I'm not saying it's a Monk item, no Jewelry type item is just a "Monk item" but it's surely nice for us given that incredible AC and the 100 HP's don't hurt either.

In regards to "Wasting 100 mana on a Monk"... I can make far greater use of 100 HP's than a high level caster will likely make with 100 mana so it's a fairly even trade. Further more, Monks need mana too! For what? Search me, but I want my mana pool.

Ultimately, it's a sweet item for most anybody that can get their hands on it. I'll be packing my loaded dice if this 'en drops ;)

▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 10:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250383876
11 posts
This would be good item cause of the AC,HP, Resist. Maybe that is everything it does.
▲ Page top
Class Fighting
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 11:03 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757749565
Sage
629 posts
LOL, I guess I understand people fighting over the various "Proper" usage of a particular item, but this item, it seems, was intended simply to be an Uber resist item and AC bonus for ANY class, and the HP and MP are just cake, if you can use them. If you're in Veeshan's Peak, 100 HP doesn't mean jack to your survival... not the way those mobs hit, LOL. In other words, it seems to have been intended as an item that can't be obviously geared to any one class. Of course, nothing that comes out of Veeshan's Peak is intended to be useful as much as it is a status symbol that just so happens to make the incredibly uber a tad moreso. ^_^ Just my 2cp. ^_^
▲ Page top
RE: Class Fighting
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 12:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763446526
48 posts
One HP is the difference between life and death. If you can get 5 items that have +100 HP on them, you have added 500 HP to your character, and in most fights, even in major battles, thats at least one round.

The other fact is that most high end battles are against dragons, and for most of those fights, you need multiple resists. Very few items in the game give you +10 to all resists and still give you good AC. This is one of those items.

Anyone that is lucky enough to be chosen to loot this item will be quite happy. And it is a good item for everyone.
▲ Page top
Wow
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 7:31 AM Rating: Excellent

Scholar

every one should be drooling for this item 10 to every resist is useful by all classes and the 30 ac is more useful to mele classes however the 100 hp/mana is useful by all you cant say this is an item for hybrids its an item for everyone

right
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 6:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757652085
81 posts
ok, now you're getting annoying. Why would anyone waste 100 mana on a monk? Yes, its 0.0 weight, but that doesn't always make it a monk item.
▲ Page top
RE: right
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 8:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248115978
10 posts
True the 100 mana would go to waste on a monk but thats why there is 100 hp too, what good is that for a caster? This is great for any class.
▲ Page top
RE: right
QuoteReply
# Aug 11 2001 at 1:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248019263
10 posts
Acctually 100hp is awsome for casters. As mentioned elsewhere, most highend battles involve dealing with Area Effect damages, which require both good resits and high Hp for casters to survive long enough to use most of their mana. However, being an all/all item whoever gets this (be they tank, hybrid, or caster) should be congratulated...then the monk should be shoved back into the pit to pull in another Beastie... ;P

Tyna (the headless mage) Angelica
DoV on Quellious
▲ Page top
RE: right
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2001 at 8:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756701976
57 posts
Also concerning HP on casters, as far as necromancers are concerned, a higher HP cap is more available mana. It simply provides more room to work with between the health to mana conversion spells, and lifetaps.

Please, get your facts straight. Bard 1hs, 1hb, piercing, offense, and defense skills have the exact same cap as Paladins/Sks. We get dual weild, they get double attack.

168 posts
One word: Nerf.

FoH doesn't want any more incidents like the one involving the (still very cool) item, Mrylokar's Dagger of Blood.

You can read the full stories and conspiracy theories under that particular piercing item, but the gist of the matter was a bunch of whiners, upon looking at the original posting of this item, all cried in harmony to get it nerfed. KNOWING full well that most of them would never get, never see, and had no intentions on getting the Dagger of Blood. Now this dagger, which drops off of god-like creatures, is just a souped-up version of a Tombcarver :p

Of course, if Furor hates druids like everyone says, there's little reason why FoH wouldn't release druid-specific ToV/Sleeper's items to the general public ... so the self-same whiners can attack these new loot pinatas with their nerf-bats. ^_^

===========================================
Khemosaabe "SPAM" Longbow (of Cazic Thule server)
Lvl 51 Ranger and Slayer-of-Bunny-Wabbits
"...She says I am the one / But, the CHAIR is not my son!"
▲ Page top
RE: The Druid Armplates
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 7:38 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750976102
281 posts
ok ive read the many posts that FoH has made about the super dagger being nerfed but..... i still havnt seen one screen shot of a GM saying" well we got 100 emails from whining newbies who will never get this item today so we decided that these newbies deserve to have your item nerfed to make them feel better about this game so this is why we nerfed this item" infact ive read posts from verant that said they nerfed the item because "THEY" felt it would soon become too powerful in the game. if you realy believe that verant isnt going to know what items they have gotten off these mobs and isnt going to continue to nerf items when they feel like it you need to open your eyes.
▲ Page top
RE: The Druid Armplates
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2001 at 2:51 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592250166807
Scholar
69 posts
At this point in time, the best loot in the game comes from ToV or something (maybe ToV isn't accurate for the best area for loot - don't know, don't care). Anyway, from every subsequent expansion new and insanely better items have been added. With items in the game as good as some of them are, how much better can we expect these items to get? As far as the dagger of blood getting nerfed, yeah that sucks - but only VI knows what kind of equipment will be available in the Luclin expansion. In order to maintain their customer base, VI is either going to need to set the level limit higher, or they're going to need to add better and better equipment. The question is, how much better is it going to get? It has to be good enough to get people to buy the expansion, but it can't be SO good that its not upgradeable.
Think about it, they're not resetting the level limit in Luclin so they need to add some pretty killer equipment. But if they do raise the level limit, North Wing of ToV is going to become the new Sebilis ('level 67 Bard LFG!')
My prediction? They will always need to add better equipment, but how soon before people start selling fungi tunics to merchants because everything else in the game is just too damn good?
Sooner or later, they'll need to allow players to get to level 70 - either that, or whatever expansion comes after Luclin is going to be nearly pointless.
Look forward to seeing level 70 at some point in 2002 - the oracle has spoken. I don't know this from first hand, but it just seems pretty damn obvious that VI is going to need to do something to maintain their customer base. Either that, or I look forward to twinking my new little beastmaster with a 100 AC, 25 to all stats, 20 vs everything, right-clickable instant CHeal effect BP in the post-Luclin expansion.
How does this relate to the nerfed dagger of blood? I honestly don't think it was n00bs that got the dagger nerfed - I think it was the fact that for Luclin to top it, they would've had to come out with a 13/15 dagger (add stats/proc)- and they just weren't ready for someone to have that - not yet anyway.

I hope luclin is the last and they make a everquest 2 new world or the same i dont care.

We have every confidence that the item was nerfed due to newbie whining alone. If you knew the loot table of every mob in North ToV along with their respective difficulties, you would agree as well.

The only group of people currently stupid enough to be releasing Vulak loot is the gimp guild Defenders of Akanon, which used a design flaw to kill Vulak, and does not understand nor appreciate why Vulak loot has not been spoiled. They haven't killed most of the dragons in the north wing, and killing Vulak was due to a loophole in mob AI.

__DEL__1591756701976

100 mana would make a great deal of difference for a bard in a soloing environment, for sure. The higher level charm song uses mana on a continual basis, and medding up is a slow and painful process because bards have a skill cap of 1 in meditate.
